opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My lower back has completely seized up, the pain is agonizing, and I haven't been able to get out of this chair for 4 hours.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain came on suddenly when I tried to stand up and now I physically can't move — I'm terrified I've slipped a disc or done something serious to my spine.

symptom_narrative.onset: About 4 hours ago — I'd been sitting on the sofa for a long time, went to stand up, and my whole lower back locked up in a violent spasm.
symptom_narrative.duration: 4 hours and I still can't move
symptom_narrative.character: A deep, locked-up, aching pain across the base of my back and both sides of my pelvis — it feels like the muscles have clamped down solid.
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: Agonizing — honestly one of the worst pains I've had. I have to hold my breath when I try to shift position.
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: Low down in the back and across both sides, around the top of the buttocks/pelvis.
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: Constant since it seized up; every attempt to move sets off the spasm.
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: Sitting completely still. An anti-inflammatory tablet has taken the edge off in the past.
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: Any movement — trying to stand, bend or twist.
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: Just the back and the muscle spasm. I feel a bit panicky because I can't move.
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: My back plays up on and off — I've got a long-standing inflammatory back problem — but it's never locked me up this badly before.
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't get out of the chair, and I live on my own so I'm worried about even getting to the bathroom.

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: A long-standing inflammatory back condition — I was told it's linked to a gene (HLA-B27 positive) and that my back X-rays have always come back normal.
past_medical_history.how_managed: Anti-inflammatory tablets now and then when it flares.

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Lives alone; currently stuck in a chair and worried about being able to move around the house safely.

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: Fears a catastrophic spinal injury (slipped/ruptured disc, nerve or cord damage) and wants urgent reassurance it is not permanent.

objective_data_offered.value: I can't stand up out of the chair — my back locks and goes into spasm the second I try to move.
objective_data_offered.timing: right now, for the last 4 hours

objective_data_offered.value: I have to hold my breath and brace whenever I try to shift my weight because it hurts so much.
